skill.name: video-prompting-guide
skill.description: Guidance for writing effective AI video generation prompts with clear shot, subject, motion, lighting, style, and technical details.
skill.summary: Create structured AI video prompts for cinematic, product, lifestyle, and model-specific workflows.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: AI review confirms a high-risk installer pattern: SKILL.md line 16 tells users to pipe a remote curl response into sh. Most external-command findings are fenced markdown examples for infsh or npx, but they still show the skill encourages networked CLI execution. The weak-cryptography findings appear to be false positives from SHA-256 text or ordinary prompt language, with no evidence of cryptographic code.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: SKILL.md line 16 instructs users to run curl against https://cli.inference.sh and pipe the response directly into sh. This is dangerous because a changed or intercepted installer can execute arbitrary code before the user reviews it. Line 24 documents checksum verification, but the quick start command itself still executes remote content immediately.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a direct curl-to-sh installer pattern with a public URL. The surrounding install note reduces evidence of malicious intent but does not remove the execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Skill Encourages Networked CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The guide contains multiple fenced command examples that run infsh apps and install related skills with npx. These examples are relevant to the skill, but they can trigger remote service calls, transmit prompts to inference.sh-backed models, or install additional community skills.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in markdown examples and match the stated video-generation purpose. Risk remains because execution would contact external services or add more skills.

low_findings.title: External Documentation And Image URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh documentation, an external hosted image, the CLI installer, the distribution host, and related documentation pages. These URLs are expected for a platform guide, but they create normal third-party network dependency and tracking exposure if opened.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are directly present and are not hidden. They appear informational except where the installer URL is also used by the high-risk shell command.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations do not show cryptographic implementation or insecure hashing use. Line 24 refers to SHA-256 checksum verification, and other flagged lines are ordinary guide text or prompt examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing or encryption code at the flagged locations. The scanner likely matched substrings inside prose such as SHA-256 or prompt vocabulary.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Match Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reported system reconnaissance location is a Common Mistakes section heading and table, not host discovery logic. No evidence found of commands that enumerate the local machine, users, processes, or network interfaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible prose about prompt-writing mistakes. The confidence is high for dismissal because no executable reconnaissance command appears there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe To Shell Installation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start command uses curl output as shell input, which can execute unreviewed remote code on a user system.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit on the cited line. It is a recognized high-risk installer style even when used by legitimate projects.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed External CLI Command Surface
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill front matter allows Bash commands matching infsh, enabling the assistant to run a networked video-generation CLI if available.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is direct evidence. The risk depends on runtime approval and credentials, so it is not treated as malicious by itself.

content.value_statement: AI video models often produce weak results when prompts lack camera, lighting, motion, and style details. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured guide for writing clearer video generation prompts.
actual_capabilities: Explains a prompt structure using shot type, subject, action, setting, lighting, style, and technical details.
Provides shot type, camera movement, lighting, style, and quality keyword references.
Includes example prompts for product, documentary, lifestyle, food, and futuristic video concepts.
Gives model-specific tips for Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok video workflows.
Shows an iterative method for improving a simple video prompt with more visual detail.
limitations: It is a prompting guide, not a video generation engine by itself.
Examples rely on inference.sh CLI commands and may require an account or installed CLI.
Model behavior can change, so prompt results may vary across providers and versions.
It does not validate content policy, copyright, licensing, or brand safety for generated videos.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Video Concepts
use_cases.description: Turn product goals into prompts with camera movement, studio lighting, and commercial-quality visual direction.

use_cases.title: Draft Cinematic Scene Prompts
use_cases.description: Describe shot type, mood, motion, and technical style before testing a scene in a video model.

use_cases.title: Improve Prompt Iterations
use_cases.description: Refine vague video ideas into specific prompts that are easier to compare across models.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write one AI video prompt for this idea: [idea]. Include subject, action, setting, lighting, and style.

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Rewrite
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this video prompt using the guide structure. Add shot type, camera movement, lighting, pacing, and technical details: [prompt].

prompt_templates.title: Model-Specific Version
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this video concept for [Veo, Seedance, Wan, or Grok]. Explain the prompt choices and keep it to one scene: [concept].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Prompt Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three distinct AI video prompts for [campaign or scene]. Vary camera language, lighting, style, motion, and technical keywords.

output_examples.input: A simple prompt for a coffee brand video.
output_examples.output: A close-up tracking shot of espresso pouring into a white ceramic cup, morning sunlight through a cafe window, soft steam, warm color grade, shallow depth of field, cinematic 4K.

output_examples.input: Improve: a person walking in a forest.
output_examples.output: A medium tracking shot of a person in a red coat walking through an autumn forest, golden hour light through leaves, gentle handheld motion, film grain, calm cinematic mood.

output_examples.input: Create three prompt directions for a futuristic dashboard reveal.
output: Smooth dolly-in toward a transparent dashboard, blue rim lighting, reflections on glass, precise sci-fi interface details.
Wide static shot of a dark control room as holographic panels activate one by one, low key lighting, slow atmospheric pacing.
Handheld close-up of fingers swiping across a glowing interface, shallow depth of field, cyan highlights, energetic tech launch style.

best_practices: Use one clear scene per prompt before adding complex edits or transitions.
Specify camera movement, lighting, subject action, setting, and visual style together.
Iterate from a simple prompt to a detailed version and compare outputs systematically.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague requests such as a nice video without visual details.
Do not combine conflicting styles unless the contrast is intentional and clearly described.
Do not rely on quality keywords alone when the subject, action, and camera are missing.

faq.question: Does this skill generate videos directly?
faq.answer: No. It helps write prompts and includes example CLI commands for external video generation tools.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this guide?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Which video models are covered?
faq.answer: The guide mentions Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok, Kling, Runway, Pika, and Sora prompting concepts.

faq.question: Can it help with product videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes product demo prompt examples with camera, lighting, and commercial style details.

faq.question: Does it include negative prompts?
faq.answer: The skill description mentions negative prompts, but the visible guide focuses mainly on positive prompt structure and examples.

faq.question: Why is the security rating high?
faq.answer: The quick start includes a curl-to-sh installer command, which can execute remote code before review.
